evidence.claim: NotebookLM ROI evidence contradicts optimistic migration: aggregators still dominate mobile ordering, customers value marketplace convenience/discounts, direct shift may require incentives, and no verified independent-restaurant payback period exists.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Delivery can consume commission savings through rider visa/licensing/fuel/Salik/fleet costs, outsourced per-drop fees, failed delivery refunds, SLA gaps, and restaurant management burden.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: ROI must separate incremental orders, shifted marketplace orders, structured phone/WhatsApp conversion, pickup, delivery, and catering; shifted orders produce margin delta, not incremental revenue.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Provider economics require setup fees, recurring support, usage caps, pass-through messaging/payment/AI costs, and custom quotes for integrations; low flat subscriptions with uncapped support are unsafe.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Value paths beyond commission savings include AOV/basket improvement, structured ordering, repeat activation, first-party data, catering enquiries, pickup, reduced errors, and analytics, but evidence strength varies.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: WhatsApp, QR/table ordering, CRM/loyalty, dispatch, POS, payments, analytics, and AI assistants can be modules, but risk and third-party cost differ enough to avoid bundling all by default.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Direct-ordering SaaS competes with WordPress/Wix, WhatsApp/phone, social/Google, POS ecosystems, agencies, and marketplaces; each is better under different budget, readiness, and operational conditions.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Restaurants should retain marketplaces for discovery and some delivery demand while targeting selective repeat-customer migration to owned channels; total marketplace replacement is commercially unsound.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Evidence supports simple tiers plus paid add-ons, annual/recurring support, transparent pass-through costs, usage caps, and custom-quote triggers for multi-branch, API, POS, delivery, and AI complexity.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Early deployments should default to owned ordering basics and manual/pickup workflows; payments, WhatsApp API, delivery dispatch, POS, AI, and complex hosting/integration dependencies should be optional, pilot-only, or custom-quote.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Current inspected sources support marketplace commission planning range of roughly 15%-35%, with effective costs higher when payment, delivery, promotions, and ads are included.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Delivery economics require per-drop, fixed rider, and hybrid break-even modeling; current inspected pages support Careem Express capability but not public per-order price tables.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Restaurant website/order SaaS retention risk is material because low-ARPA SMB SaaS churn and Dubai restaurant churn can undermine provider payback.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Customer preference evidence favors convenience, discounts, tracking, stored payment, and marketplace trust; direct channel must compete on repeat convenience and value, not moral appeal alone.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Stripe UAE standard online card pricing is 2.9% + AED 1 per domestic-card transaction, plus international/currency extras and AED 60 dispute fees.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: WhatsApp Business Platform pricing is usage-based and per-message after the 2025 model change; exact UAE rates should be taken from Meta/BSP quote before sale.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Telegram Bot API is available as an official bot interface and has no comparable per-message platform fee found in inspected official docs; hosting and development still cost money.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Rider/fleet planning should use wide ranges because visa, license, fuel, Salik, insurance, maintenance, replacement, and SLA costs vary materially by model and contract.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: CRM/loyalty/first-party data are medium-term value drivers only when paired with opt-in capture, segmentation, campaigns, and repeat-order mechanisms.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Digital ordering may increase AOV through menu design, upsell, and repeat convenience, but uplift claims are mostly vendor/global and should be scenario variables.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Catering/group-order modules are credible for restaurants with family trays, office meals, Ramadan/group demand, or event enquiries, but enquiries must not be counted as closed sales.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Arabic/English web presence is strategically useful in UAE customer contexts, but conversion uplift should not be quantified without pilot data.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Website-builder and agency alternatives create a low-to-high price spectrum: DIY builders are cheap, CMS/freelancer builds add support risk, bespoke agencies are expensive.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Restaurant ordering SaaS competitors commonly charge monthly subscriptions, transaction fees, or custom quotes; this sets a market ceiling for simple ordering products.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Foodics/Sapaad/Syrve/Deliverect-style ecosystems bundle POS, online ordering, QR, loyalty, analytics, and integrations; they are alternatives for mature operators, not substitutes for a tiny entry product.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Google Business Profile, Instagram, WhatsApp Business app, QR/static menus, and phone orders remain valid low-cost alternatives for restaurants lacking readiness for full direct-ordering workflows.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Cloudflare, Supabase, domains, and OpenAI costs are small but nonzero at low traffic; they must be separated from builder fees or included within capped hosting allowances.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: SMS, WhatsApp, and AI usage costs should be pass-through or capped allowances; flat unlimited usage is margin-dangerous.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Delivery integrations should be custom-quote or pilot-only until provider price, API access, SLA, liability, and operational fit are confirmed for the target restaurant.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: POS integrations should be compatibility-matrix driven; supported paths can be sold only when API/order-injection/menu sync access is documented and tested.
evidence.geography: UAE primary; GCC/global where UAE unavailable

evidence.claim: Cache-reuse attempt used stricter freshness/geography than the stored evidence and was answered by ER-0033.
evidence.geography: UAE

evidence.claim: Cache-reuse attempt used stricter freshness/geography than the stored evidence and was answered by ER-0033.
evidence.geography: UAE/GCC
